items.title: Final Fantasy XIV Collaborates With Shibuya To Give Goodies In Return For Tax Contributions

items.title: Wobbly blob hat alert: Final Fantasy XIV is rerunning its Dragon Quest X crossover event on June 25
Final Fantasy 14 Players in Japan Can Now Get In-Game Cosmetics and Items by Paying Their Real Life Taxes
items.summary: Final Fantasy 14 players in Japan now have the opportunity to get in-game cosmetics and items by paying their real life taxes, as reported by Famitsu. These rewards include the online MMORPG’s Megashiba Mount.
items.content:
Final Fantasy 14 players in Japan now have the opportunity to get in-game cosmetics and items by paying their real life taxes, as reported by Famitsu . These rewards include the online MMORPG’s Megashiba Mount.

Japan has a Hometown tax initiative (Furusato Nozei), which allows people to redirect portions of their residence tax to other areas of the country, even if they don’t live there. In return, you can choose a special gift from the region (everything from local snacks and traditional handcrafted items, to hotel stays and local cultural experiences). The scheme launched back in 2008 with the aim of directing tax funds back to rural, less-populated areas, but now almost every municipality in the country offers some kind of reward via the scheme. It’s a win-win as local governments receive more funds, while taxpayers receive presents in exchange for tax money they would have had to fork out anyway.

In recent years, some areas have started offering digital rewards. For example, last year Shibuya ward offered in-game currency in the popular mobile games Monster Strike and Kotodaman (as reported by Automaton ).

Now, Shibuya ward and Square Enix have teamed up to offer rewards for the online MMORPG Final Fantasy 14. As the special site shows, players in Japan can choose between six different sets of in-game items and cosmetics to purchase with their residence tax. Namely, three costume sets priced at 7,000 yen (approx. $43) each: the Majitek Attire, Collegiate Attire (Skirt), and Collegiate Attire (Slacks) sets), four dance emotes for 11,000 yen (approx. $68), a set of five Phials of Fantasia for 17,000 yen (approx. $105), and the Megashiba Mount for 9,000 yen (approx. $56).

Final Fantasy 14 players outside Japan needn’t feel too left out though, as these items can be purchased from the Final Fantasy 14 online store .

Some of the Final Fantasy 14 rewards on offer sync up nicely with this area of Tokyo. The Megashiba mount is a giant, rideable dog. Shibuya is famous for Hachiko, a dog who loyally waited for his owner outside of Shibuya Station, even after his master passed away. There is a statue dedicated to the patient pup outside the station, and the true story has inspired a couple of movie adaptations, including Hachi: A Dog’s Tale , starring Richard Gere.

Although Hachiko was a different Japanese dog breed (an Akita rather than a Shiba Inu), it seems likely that Shibuya Ward and Square Enix decided to include the Megashiba Mount in the Hometown tax scheme as a reference to Shibuya’s famous dog. The Collegiate Attire Costumes also mesh well with Shibuya, as with its fashion stores, live music venues, cafes and night clubs it’s known as an area for youth culture.

Verity Townsend is a Japan-based freelance writer who previously served as editor, contributor and translator for the game news site Automaton West. She has also written about Japanese culture and movies for various publications.

items.title: Japan is Giving Final Fantasy Goodies Away if You Pay Your Taxes
items.summary: Final Fantasy XIV is turning tax season into a win for MMORPG fans in Japan.
items.content:
Japan is Giving Final Fantasy Goodies Away if You Pay Your Taxes From DualShockers (Written by Tay Garcia) on June 18, 2026 | OpenCritic

Video games have a unique way of teaching us some valuable real-world skills, whether we realize it or not. Managing inventory in an RPG forces us to learn how to prioritize our resources, coordinating raids in an MMO builds teamwork, horror titles teach us to manage our anxiety, and strategy games can practically be history lessons sometimes.

But while we are used to games pushing us to sharpen our minds without us even noticing it, Square Enix is simply taking that concept to a whole new level by literally rewarding you for keeping your real-life finances and civic duties in check.

That is because the Japanese company is partnering with government authorities in Japan to turn tax season into a win for MMORPG fans – especially those who love to venture in Eorzea.

Paying Taxes Never Felt so Rewarding (in Japan)

According to Famitsu , Square Enix has officially started offering highly desirable in-game rewards for Final Fantasy XIV players who participate in the hometown tax donation program for Tokyo’s famous Shibuya ward.

Their headquarters are famously located right in the heart of Shibuya, and the studio is using this initiative to give back to the local community while...

items.title: Final Fantasy 14 rewards Japanese players for paying their taxes: make a donation and get a pile of MMO cosmetics
items.content:
Granted, it's a lot more than their in-game store price in taxes to get them all

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Square Enix) Copy link Facebook X Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Square Enix is incentivizing Final Fantasy 14 players to pay their taxes promptly by offering in-game cosmetics for the MMO.

As reported by Famitsu (translated and reported by Automaton ), Tokyo's Shibuya Ward is offering in-game items to Japanese Final Fantasy 14 players for doing their taxes. Japan's "Furusato Nozei" system allows citizens to donate a portion of income tax to a specific city, as opposed to it all being funneled in the one place, with chosen areas offering incentives to donate to them. This year, Tokyo's Shibuya Ward is teaming up with Square Enix to offer items in Final Fantasy 14.

Through donations, players can choose a number of items, such as a five pack of Fantasia Potions (which allow you to edit your character) for 17,000 Yen, a Megashiba Inu mount for 9,000 Yen, and a Magitek Costume set for 7,000 Yen.

To get all six gift packs you'd need to donate 58,000 Yen (roughly $362). As Automaton points out, the actual in-game price of the same items is estimated around $88, so they're really more of a bonus for your donation to the cause. That's a pretty steep jump for the cosmetics alone, so maybe don't plan on moving to Japan just for them.

But if you're already there and paying your taxes anyway, then there's a way to get some 'free' stuff in the critically acclaimed MMORPG with a free trial that now runs through– alright, I'll stop. Thankfully, these items don't seem to be tied to this incentive. Let's hope it stays that way and we don't see a world with tax exclusives

items.title: This Final Fantasy game is offering players rewards for doing their taxes
items.summary: The free goodies include in-game cosmetics and a giant dog companion that can be used as a mount.
items.content:
By Elijah Gonzalez Published Jun 17, 2026, 11:16 AM EDT News They'll also receive in-game costumes and Phials of Fantasia

Capcom pulls Dragon's Dogma 2's controversial microtransactions ahead of DLC launch All active Heartopia codes for free items Crimson Desert patch turns the game into a total pet paradise Japanese Final Fantasy 14 players can earn an adorable giant dog by paying their taxes Image: Square Enix Final Fantasy 14 Sign in to your Polygon.com account Final Fantasy 14 devotees rejoice: you can now earn $88 worth of in-game cosmetics by paying your taxes. Well, if you live in Japan, anyway. Courtesy of Famitsu , Square Enix has teamed up with Tokyo’s Shibuya Ward to offer digital items to Japanese citizens who contribute part of their taxes to the district.

Japan has what’s called the hometown tax contribution system , which allows participants to allocate part of their income taxes to a specific town of their choice, even if they don't currently live there. The initiative was primarily created to help support rural communities suffering from depopulation. When a town or city receives these tax allocations, they tend to send the contributor gifts, such as local specialty goods, or in this case, nerd stuff.

In recent years, some communities have taken to offering digital awards, like how Shibuya Ward previously partnered with the mobile games Monster Strike and Kotodaman . For those who haven’t heard of Shibuya, it’s a famous part of Tokyo traditionally known for its overlap with otaku culture, including anime and video games.

As for the specific Final Fantasy 14 items offered as a reward for Shibuya tax contributions, players will receive five Phials of Fantasia (these are used to change your characters’ appearance), the Collegiate Attire slacks and skirt, the Magitek costume, four dance emotes, and best of all, the Megashiba mount. This large dog is a reference to the Shibuya mascot Hachiko, an Akita Inu that earned a bronze statue for its Argos-like loyalty to its owner. While the items offered aren't exclusive to those who contribute to Shibuya, the ward is likely to see at least a few dedicated FF14 heads sending their taxes for these e-goods.

Earlier this month, Final Fantasy 14 received its 7.51 patch, which added new Ultimate raid challenges featuring Final Fantasy 6 antagonist Kefka Palazzo; it's the second of four updates that Square has promised for this year. The game’s previous major expansion, Dawntrail , launched in 2024, and its upcoming add-on Evercold is set for 2027. Additionally, the game is coming to the Nintendo Switch 2 this August.

items.title: Final Fantasy XIV Items Now Offered As Tax Rewards
items.summary: Shibuya is offering Final Fantasy XIV items as a reward when donating to it via the hometown tax system in Japan.
items.content:
People who donate to Shibuya through the hometown tax system can now receive in-game rewards in Final Fantasy XIV . There are six different rewards that you can get, and what you receive is based on how much money you donate.

To receive your hometown tax donation reward, you can use this website . First, you choose which item you want (and how much you want to donate), and then you’ll go to the item page for it. Once you purchase it, you can get the serial code to redeem your reward in-game.

Here are the rewards and how much money you’ll need to give to receive them:

For reference, the hometown tax system in Japan lets taxpayers in urban cities ‌contribute to rural areas for a tax credit. It was created in response to people who leave their hometowns to work or study in the city, thus paying taxes there and giving nothing back to their hometowns, even though they had used public services there while growing up. To entice people to donate money, many cities offer gifts like food. As a note, some other popular perks from Shibuya include dog pee mats (really), a food processor, and cooking pot.

Final Fantasy XIV is readily available on the PS4, PS5, Xbox Series X, and Windows PC. The Switch 2 version will come out in August 2026.

items.title: Japanese citizens can now get free Final Fantasy 14 items by paying their taxes
items.summary: People paying their taxes in Japan will be eligible for freebie Final Fantasy XIV cosmetics under the country's Furusato Nozei system.
items.content:
Home News Japanese citizens can now get free Final Fantasy 14 items by paying their taxes People paying their taxes in Japan will be eligible for freebie Final Fantasy XIV cosmetics under the country's Furusato Nozei system.

News 2026-06-17 22:00 Carlos "Zoto" Zotomayor 2 Tokyo’s Shibuya Ward has begun offering free in-game items to Japanese Final Fantasy XIV players as a reward for their tax contributions (source: Famitsu ). These purely cosmetic items, which can also be purchased with real money, are part of Square Enix’s effort to deepen its ties with the local playerbase.

The collaboration is part of Japan’s “Furusato Nozei” (hometown tax contribution) system, which allows you to donate a portion of your income tax to a specific city (such as your hometown or a place with personal significance) instead of paying it all to the central government. In return, your chosen city will send you gifts. These most often include local specialty goods like fresh produce, rice, or wagyu beef, but recently, there have been some unconventional options like NFTs and digital awards appearing, too.

Last year, Shibuya Ward collaborated with Japanese mobile games Monster Strike and Kotodaman to start providing in-game currency to taxpayers as a possible reward to opt for. This year, it is teaming up with Square Enix to gift five Phials of Fantasia (an item that allows you to change your character’s appearance, race, and gender), the Collegiate Attire (Skirt) and the Collegiate Attire (Slacks) costume sets, the Magitek attire, four dance emotes, and a Megashiba mount (a reference to Shibuya Station’s own Hachiko statue) to players for free. Based on their listed prices on the Final Fantasy XIV Online Store , the reward package has an estimated value of at least $88 USD.

While Square Enix headquarters isn’t in Shibuya, it does have an office there. As such, the company says it will “continue to undertake various initiatives while capitalizing on Shibuya’s unique characteristics.”

Related: Square Enix takes action against Final Fantasy 14 video content creator over harassment of employees following shutdown of defamatory news blog

Carlos "Zoto" Zotomayor Automaton West writer. Zoto has been playing video games for 30+ years now but has only recently come to grips with PC gaming. When he isn't playing video games, he watches romance anime and gets mad when his best girl never wins.

items.content:
https://store.steampowered.com/app/3259780/ What if FINAL FANTASY had continued to push the limits of pixel art and evolved from its classic roots? The first FINAL FANTASY to join the HD‑2D lineup, FINAL FANTASY RESONANCE delivers a visually striking experience that feels both nostalgic and new, enhanced by dynamic camera angles and pixelized 3D models that make every moment more dramatic and immersive. Return to the pixel‑art world of FINAL FANTASY, evolved from its classic roots. Travel with companions bound by fate and call on Visions to aid you in battle to uncover the truth behind a force that threatens to bring everything to ruin.

Digital Deluxe Edition:

This product is a bundle that includes the FINAL FANTASY RESONANCE base game along with the Magitek & Grimoire Deluxe Pack, which contains an item that transforms your chocobo's appearance into magitek armor, an item exclusive to this pack that unlocks a certain character's Fel Meteor Limit Burst, and useful items to aid you at the start of your journey. items.title: Square Enix Releases Final Fantasy XIV Online Switch 2 Gameplay Trailer
items.content:
During the Nintendo Direct this week, Square Enix's upcoming release Final Fantasy XIV Online featured in a sizzle reel towards the end of the presentation.

Although it was only on display for a moment, it was arguably the best look at this version of the game yet. Now, Square Enix has released a gameplay trailer of the online multiplayer title running on the Switch 2. It's got some quick segments of gameplay, mixed with in-game cutscenes.

In case you missed it, this title will be getting an "early access" release on the Switch 2 in August 2026 . Here are some additional details from Square Enix's support page about the early access period, or if you're a player coming across from another platform:

Those playing FINAL FANTASY XIV for the first time on Nintendo Switch 2 Purchase FINAL FANTASY XIV for Nintendo Switch 2 and a subscription to play the game.

Current FINAL FANTASY XIV players who wish to play on Nintendo Switch 2 If you already play FINAL FANTASY XIV on another platform, you will still need to purchase FINAL FANTASY XIV for Nintendo Switch 2 and a separate subscription for the Nintendo Switch 2 version of the game. However, if you have remaining game time on other platforms, the Nintendo Switch 2 subscription will be available at half price.

There will be an approximately one-month period of early access to ensure server stability, after which official service will start. A subscription to Nintendo Switch Online will not be required to play FINAL FANTASY XIV on Nintendo Switch 2 in either the early access period or during official service.

During the latest Nintendo Direct, Square Enix also announced the new HD-2D title, Final Fantasy Resonance for Switch and Switch 2. It's out this October.

Players will require a separate subscription from other platforms

items.title: Patch 7.51 Notes
items.summary: Patch 7.51 introduces the seventh installment of the ultimate raid series, Dancing Mad (Ultimate), as well as new custom deliveries featuring Tiisol Ja. And for our intrepid Disciples of the Hand and Land, new exploration awaits on Auxesia.
items.content:
Patch 7.51 introduces the seventh installment of the ultimate raid series, Dancing Mad (Ultimate), as well as new custom deliveries featuring Tiisol Ja. And for our intrepid Disciples of the Hand and Land, new exploration awaits on Auxesia. * Additional features will be implemented in future updates:
  • The Occult Crescent: North Horn, Weapon Enhancement Quests - Phantom Weapons, Inconceivably Further Hildibrand Adventures sidequests, and Allied Society capstone sidequests are scheduled to release in Patch 7.55.
  • Further main scenario quests and the beastmaster limited job are scheduled to release in Patch 7.56.
Read the full patch notes on the Lodestone. Launch FINAL FANTASY XIV Now

items.title: The Make It Rain Campaign Begins May 29!
items.summary: The Make It Rain Campaign returns once more to the Gold Saucer! With a 50% MGP bonus and an assortment of adorable otter-themed goods on offer, you won't want to miss out! Furthermore, campaign attendants will be offering a range of items a
items.content:
The Make It Rain Campaign returns once more to the Gold Saucer! With a 50% MGP bonus and an assortment of adorable otter-themed goods on offer, you won't want to miss out! Furthermore, campaign attendants will be offering a range of items at discounted prices, including some exclusive limited-time deals, so be sure to join the festivities and rake in the rewards while you can!

Event Duration

From Friday, May 29, 2026 at 1:00 a.m. (PDT) / 8:00 (GMT) / 9:00 (BST) / 18:00 (AEST) to Wednesday, June 24, 2026 at 7:59 a.m. (PDT) / 14:59 (GMT) / 15:59 (BST) / Thursday, 25 June at 0:59 (AEST)

How to Participate

Begin the quest "You Otter Be There" by speaking to Ollier in Ul’dah - Steps of Nald (X:9.4 Y:9.1). * Players must be level 15 and above to start the quest. * Players must first complete the quest "It Could Happen to You."

Rewards

* MGP rewards from the Gold Saucer have been increased by 50% for the duration of this event. * Certain items available during previous iterations of the event can be obtained via the campaign attendant. * The Crystal Tower striker, Monster Toss machine, Cuff-a-Cur machine, and a number of other prizes may be purchased at a reduced MGP cost from the campaign attendant. Visit the special site for more details.

items.title: Play Free for up to 4 Days in the Latest Free Login Campaign!
items.summary: In the hope that would-be adventurers can experience all that FFXIV has to offer, we are pleased to announce the latest Free Login Campaign. Follow the Trail to the Heavens ! In Patch 7.5, you can face Enuo in the Unmaking, take a stroll th
items.content:
In the hope that would-be adventurers can experience all that FFXIV has to offer, we are pleased to announce the latest Free Login Campaign. Follow the Trail to the Heavens! In Patch 7.5, you can face Enuo in the Unmaking, take a stroll through Windurst: The Third Walk in the FINAL FANTASY XI alliance raid crossover, and leap into battle in Archeia Harmonias, an all-new arena for Crystalline Conflict. A host of other additions and adjustments will make your time in Eorzea more streamlined and enjoyable, including increased furnishing placement limits, the consolidation of dyes, and additional pieces of gear that can now be converted to outfit glamour or stored in the armoire. With the introduction of Patch 7.5, the Shadowbringers expansion can now be played as part of the FINAL FANTASY XIV Starter Edition. The expansion will be automatically registered to your account if you already own the Starter Edition, so why not take this opportunity to jump into the adventure?

If you or any of your friends are currently taking a break from your adventures, you won't want to miss out on this limited-time event! Visit the Free Login Campaign website for details.

items.title: The Maiden's Rhapsody Returns!
items.summary: Experience the intriguing tale bridging Hydaelyn and Vana'diel as The Maiden's Rhapsody─a past collaboration with FFXI─returns to the realm once more! Embark on a journey with one of Vana'diel's heroes and discover what twists of fate broug
items.content:
Experience the intriguing tale bridging Hydaelyn and Vana'diel as The Maiden's Rhapsody─a past collaboration with FFXI─returns to the realm once more! Embark on a journey with one of Vana'diel's heroes and discover what twists of fate brought her to our realm. * This event is identical to the one previously held. * If you have already completed the event, you can replay the quests via the seasonal event replay feature. We highly recommend learning more about this feature on the collaboration special site.

Event Duration

From Friday, May 8, 2026 at 1:00 a.m. (PDT) / 8:00 (GMT) / 9:00 (BST) / 18:00 (AEST) to Monday, May 25, 2026 at 7:59 a.m. (PDT) / 14:59 (GMT) / 15:59 (BST) / Tuesday, 26 May at 00:59 (AEST)

How to Participate

Begin the quest "A Journey to Remember" by speaking to the Remumu in Limsa Lominsa - Upper Decks (X:11.6 Y:11.2).

Rewards

Visit the special site for more details.

items.title: Free trial now to include FINAL FANTASY XIV: Shadowbringers!
items.summary: Beginning Tuesday, April 28, with the release of Patch 7.5, the free trial will also include the award-winning FINAL FANTASY XIV: Shadowbringers. Spread the word and tell your friends, because there’s never been a better time to begin their
items.content: Beginning Tuesday, April 28, with the release of Patch 7.5, the free trial will also include the award-winning FINAL FANTASY XIV: Shadowbringers. Spread the word and tell your friends, because there’s never been a better time to begin their adventure! Check out the free trial. For those who have already registered FINAL FANTASY XIV: A Realm Reborn to their accounts, worry not─this change also applies to all existing owners as well. * Service accounts will be updated to reflect this change starting on Tuesday, April 28. Please note that the registration status update may not be displayed immediately on the Mog Station.
